     1→import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2→import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
     3→import { checkAuth, unauthorizedResponse } from "@/lib/auth";
     4→
     5→// interface CSVRow {
     6→//   title: string;
     7→//   url: string;
     8→//   category: string;
     9→//   description?: string;
    10→//   author?: string;
    11→// }
    12→//
    13→interface UploadResult {
    14→  success: boolean;
    15→  title: string;
    16→  message: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→export async function POST(request: Request) {
    20→  // CHECK AUTH FIRST!
    21→  if (!checkAuth(request)) {
    22→    return unauthorizedResponse();
    23→  }
    24→  try {
    25→    const body = await request.json();
    26→    const { csvContent } = body;
    27→
    28→    if (!csvContent || typeof csvContent !== "string") {
    29→      return NextResponse.json(
    30→        { error: "CSV content is required" },
    31→        { status: 400 },
    32→      );
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    // Parse CSV with semicolon separator
    36→    const lines = csvContent.trim().split("\n");
    37→
    38→    if (lines.length === 0) {
    39→      return NextResponse.json({ error: "CSV file is empty" }, { status: 400 });
    40→    }
    41→
    42→    const results: UploadResult[] = [];
    43→    let successCount = 0;
    44→    let failureCount = 0;
    45→    let startIndex = 0;
    46→
    47→    // Check if first line is a header (contains "title")
    48→    const firstLine = lines[0].toLowerCase();
    49→    if (firstLine.includes("title") && firstLine.includes("url")) {
    50→      startIndex = 1; // Skip header row
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    // Process each line
    54→    for (let i = startIndex; i < lines.length; i++) {
    55→      const line = lines[i].trim();
    56→
    57→      // Skip empty lines
    58→      if (!line) {
    59→        continue;
    60→      }
    61→
    62→      try {
    63→        // Parse CSV line with semicolon separator
    64→        const columns = line.split(";").map((col) => col.trim());
    65→
    66→        // Validate minimum required fields
    67→        if (columns.length < 3) {
    68→          results.push({
    69→            success: false,
    70→            title: columns[0] || `Row ${i + 1}`,
    71→            message:
    72→              "Missing required fields (need at least: title, url, category)",
    73→          });
    74→          failureCount++;
    75→          continue;
    76→        }
    77→
    78→        const [title, url, category, description = "", author = ""] = columns;
    79→
    80→        // Validate required fields
    81→        if (!title) {
    82→          results.push({
    83→            success: false,
    84→            title: `Row ${i + 1}`,
    85→            message: "Title is required",
    86→          });
    87→          failureCount++;
    88→          continue;
    89→        }
    90→
    91→        if (!url) {
    92→          results.push({
    93→            success: false,
    94→            title: title,
    95→            message: "URL is required",
    96→          });
    97→          failureCount++;
    98→          continue;
    99→        }
   100→
   101→        if (!category) {
   102→          results.push({
   103→            success: false,
   104→            title: title,
   105→            message: "Category is required",
   106→          });
   107→          failureCount++;
   108→          continue;
   109→        }
   110→
   111→        // Validate URL format
   112→        try {
   113→          new URL(url);
   114→        } catch {
   115→          results.push({
   116→            success: false,
   117→            title: title,
   118→            message: "Invalid URL format",
   119→          });
   120→          failureCount++;
   121→          continue;
   122→        }
   123→
   124→        // Create story in database
   125→        await prisma.story.create({
   126→          data: {
   127→            title: title,
   128→            url: url,
   129→            category: category,
   130→            description: description || null,
   131→            author: author || null,
   132→            timestamp: new Date(),
   133→          },
   134→        });
   135→
   136→        results.push({
   137→          success: true,
   138→          title: title,
   139→          message: "Successfully added",
   140→        });
   141→        successCount++;
   142→      } catch (error) {
   143→        const errorMessage =
   144→          error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error occurred";
   145→        results.push({
   146→          success: false,
   147→          title: `Row ${i + 1}`,
   148→          message: `Failed to add: ${errorMessage}`,
   149→        });
   150→        failureCount++;
   151→      }
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    return NextResponse.json({
   155→      results,
   156→      successCount,
   157→      failureCount,
   158→      totalProcessed: successCount + failureCount,
   159→    });
   160→  } catch (error) {
   161→    console.error("Error processing CSV:", error);
   162→    return NextResponse.json(
   163→      {
   164→        error: "Failed to process CSV file",
   165→        details: error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error",
   166→      },
   167→      { status: 500 },
   168→    );
   169→  }
   170→}
   171→
```
</details></tool-use>

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the patterns. Let me create a detailed report for the user.

## Frontend Patterns Analysis Report

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the frontend patterns used in this project:

---

### 1. Modal Components

**Location:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/components/`

**Key Modal Files:**
- **AddStoryModal.tsx** - Modal for creating new stories
- **EditStoryModal.tsx** - Modal for editing existing stories

**Modal Structure Pattern:**
```
Modal Container:
├── Fixed overlay backdrop (inset-0 bg-black bg-opacity-50)
├── Centered modal box (max-w-2xl max-h-[90vh] overflow-y-auto)
├── Sticky header with close button (×)
├── Form content area with consistent spacing
└── Sticky scrolling for long forms
```

**Key Features:**
- Conditionally rendered (returns null when not open)
- Props-based control: `isOpen`, `onClose`, `onSuccess`
- Modal passes data via callback props
- Overlay click doesn't close (must use close button)
- Z-index z-50 for stacking context

---

### 2. Form Handling Patterns

**State Management Approach:**
```typescript
// Single form state object
const [formData, setFormData] = useState({
  title: "",
  url: "",
  category: "",
  description: "",
  author: "",
  publicationMonth: currentMonth,
  publicationYear: currentYear,
});

// Separate UI state
const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
const [error, setError] = useState("");
const [showAuthorSuggestions, setShowAuthorSuggestions] = useState(false);
```

**Form Input Handler Pattern:**
```typescript
const handleInputChange = (
  e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement | HTMLSelectElement>
) => {
  const { name, value } = e.target;
  setFormData((prev) => ({
    ...prev,
    [name]: 
      name === "publicationMonth" || name === "publicationYear"
        ? parseInt(value)
        : value,
  }));
};
```

**Form Validation:**
- Simple inline validation before submission
- Required field checks
- Trim whitespace
- Field-level conditional parsing (e.g., month/year to parseInt)

**Error Display:**
```typescript
{error && (
  <div className="bg-red-50 text-red-600 p-3 rounded-md text-sm">
    {error}
  </div>
)}
```

**Form Submission Pattern:**
```typescript
const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  setError("");
  
  // Validation
  if (!formData.title.trim() || !formData.url.trim() || !formData.category.trim()) {
    setError("Please fill in all required fields");
    return;
  }
  
  setIsSubmitting(true);
  
  try {
    // Get auth token from localStorage
    const token = localStorage.getItem("admin_token");
    
    // API call with auth headers
    const response = await fetch("/api/stories/create", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
      },
      body: JSON.stringify(formData),
    });
    
    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error("Failed to create story");
    }
    
    // Reset form
    setFormData({ /* ... */ });
    onSuccess();
    onClose();
  } catch (err) {
    setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Failed");
  } finally {
    setIsSubmitting(false);
  }
};
```

---

### 3. Autocomplete/Dropdown Pattern

**Author Search Implementation:**
```typescript
const [showAuthorSuggestions, setShowAuthorSuggestions] = useState(false);
const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ author: "" });

const filteredAuthors = 
  formData.author.length === 0
    ? authors
    : authors.filter((author) =>
        author.toLowerCase().includes(formData.author.toLowerCase())
      );

// In render:
<input
  onFocus={() => setShowAuthorSuggestions(true)}
  onBlur={() => setTimeout(() => setShowAuthorSuggestions(false), 150)}
/>

{showAuthorSuggestions && filteredAuthors.length > 0 && (
  <div className="absolute z-10 w-full mt-1 bg-white border border-gray-300 rounded-md shadow-lg max-h-48 overflow-y-auto">
    {filteredAuthors.slice(0, 10).map((author, index) => (
      <button
        type="button"
        onMouseDown={() => selectAuthor(author)}
        className="w-full text-left px-3 py-2 hover:bg-blue-50 text-gray-900 text-sm border-b border-gray-100 last:border-0"
      >
        {/* Highlight matching text */}
      </button>
    ))}
  </div>
)}
```

---

### 4. UI Component Library

**No external UI component library used.** Instead:
- **Tailwind CSS** (v3.4.1) for all styling
- **Lucide-react** (v0.468.0) for icons
- **Custom components** using semantic HTML

**Color Scheme (Tailwind):**
- Primary: `blue-600` / `blue-700` (buttons, focus states)
- Errors: `red-50` (background) / `red-600` (text) / `red-700` (hover)
- Success: `green-50` / `green-600`
- Neutral: `gray-*` (borders, backgrounds, text)
- Backdrop: `bg-black bg-opacity-50`

**Button Patterns:**
```typescript
// Primary Button
<button className="bg-blue-600 text-white py-2 px-4 rounded-md hover:bg-blue-700 disabled:bg-gray-400 disabled:cursor-not-allowed">
  Action
</button>

// Secondary Button
<button className="px-6 py-2 border border-gray-300 text-gray-700 rounded-md hover:bg-gray-50">
  Cancel
</button>

// Icon Button
<button className="text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 p-1 rounded hover:bg-blue-50 transition-colors">
  <svg>...</svg>
</button>
```

**Input Styling:**
```typescript
className="w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 text-gray-900"
```

---

### 5. State Management

**Pattern: Local Component State with Callbacks**

```
Parent Component (AdminDashboard)
├── State: isAddModalOpen, isEditModalOpen, stories, categories, authors
├── Methods: fetchStories(), handleStoryAdded(), handleEditClick()
└── Pass callbacks to modals
    ├── AddStoryModal
    │   ├── Props: isOpen, onClose, onSuccess, categories, authors
    │   └── On submit: calls onSuccess() then onClose()
    └── EditStoryModal
        ├── Props: isOpen, onClose, onSuccess, story, categories, authors
        └── On submit: calls onSuccess() then onClose()
```

**Authentication Token:**
```typescript
const token = localStorage.getItem("admin_token");
// Used in all API requests
headers: {
  "Content-Type": "application/json",
  Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
}
```

**Session Management:**

### 6. Login Form Pattern

**Location:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/[secretPath]/login/page.tsx`

**Pattern:**
```typescript
'use client'; // Client component

const [username, setUsername] = useState('');
const [password, setPassword] = useState('');
const [error, setError] = useState('');
const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);

const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  setError('');
  setIsLoading(true);

  try {
    const response = await fetch('/api/auth', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ username, password }),
    });

    const data = await response.json();

    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(data.error || 'Login failed');
    }

    localStorage.setItem('admin_token', data.token);
    router.push(`/${secretPath}/dashboard`);
  } catch (err) {
    setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Login failed');
  } finally {
    setIsLoading(false);
  }
};
```

## Summary for Login/Signup Forms

When creating login/signup forms, follow these patterns:

1. **Structure**: Single column centered card on full-height page
2. **Form state**: Individual useState for username, password, error, isLoading
3. **Validation**: Inline before submission
4. **Submission**: Prevent default, show loading state, call API, handle response
5. **Authentication**: Store token in localStorage
6. **Styling**: Use exact same Tailwind classes (border, focus rings, hover states)
7. **Error display**: Red alert box at top of form
8. **Loading**: Disable button and show "Logging in..." text during submission
9. **Navigation**: Use `useRouter().push()` for redirects
10. **Fields**: Standard inputs with labels, required indicators
11. **Accessibility**: Use htmlFor on labels, id on inputs

The patterns are highly consistent and composable - you can mirror the modal and form patterns from AddStoryModal/EditStoryModal for signup forms.

## User Authentication System Design Plan

Based on my analysis of the codebase, here is a comprehensive implementation plan for adding user authentication to your Next.js 15 app.

---

## 1. Database Schema

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/prisma/schema.prisma`

Add the following User model and PasswordResetToken model:

```prisma
model User {
  id            String    @id @default(cuid())
  username      String    @unique
  email         String?   @unique  // Optional, for password recovery
  passwordHash  String
  createdAt     DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime  @updatedAt
  
  // Relation to favorites (replacing IP-based identification)
  favorites     UserFavorite[]
  
  @@index([email])
}

model PasswordResetToken {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  token     String   @unique
  userId    String
  expiresAt DateTime
  used      Boolean  @default(false)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@index([token])
  @@index([userId])
}

// Optional: Convert Favorite to use User relation
model UserFavorite {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  storyId   String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  story     Story    @relation(fields: [storyId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@unique([userId, storyId])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([storyId])
}
```

**Note:** The existing `Favorite` model can remain for anonymous users (IP-based), or you can migrate to `UserFavorite` for logged-in users.

---

## 2. Dependencies

Add bcrypt for password hashing and jsonwebtoken for JWT:

```bash
npm install bcrypt jsonwebtoken
npm install -D @types/bcrypt @types/jsonwebtoken
```

---

## 3. Token Strategy

### Recommended: JWT-based Authentication

**Why JWT over simple base64:**
- Cryptographically signed (tamper-proof)
- Built-in expiration
- Can contain user info (userId, username)
- Easy to differentiate from admin tokens

**Token Structure:**

```typescript
// User token payload
interface UserTokenPayload {
  type: 'user';           // Differentiates from admin
  userId: string;
  username: string;
  iat: number;            // Issued at
  exp: number;            // Expiration (7 days recommended)
}

// Admin token remains base64 for backward compatibility
// Admin token format: "admin:username:timestamp" base64 encoded
```

**Storage:**
- User tokens stored in `localStorage` as `user_token` (separate from `admin_token`)
- Or use a single `auth_token` key with type checking on the server

---

## 4. API Routes

### 4.1 POST /api/auth/signup

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/api/auth/signup/route.ts`

```typescript
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
import bcrypt from "bcrypt";
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";

const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || "your-secret-key-change-in-production";
const SALT_ROUNDS = 12;

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  try {
    const { username, password, email } = await request.json();

    // Validation
    if (!username || !password) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Username and password are required" },
        { status: 400 }
      );
    }

    if (username.length < 3 || username.length > 30) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Username must be 3-30 characters" },
        { status: 400 }
      );
    }

    if (password.length < 8) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Password must be at least 8 characters" },
        { status: 400 }
      );
    }

    // Check if username exists
    const existingUser = await prisma.user.findUnique({
      where: { username: username.toLowerCase() }
    });

    if (existingUser) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Username already taken" },
        { status: 409 }
      );
    }

    // Check if email exists (if provided)
    if (email) {
      const existingEmail = await prisma.user.findUnique({
        where: { email: email.toLowerCase() }
      });
      if (existingEmail) {
        return NextResponse.json(
          { error: "Email already registered" },
          { status: 409 }
        );
      }
    }

    // Hash password
    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, SALT_ROUNDS);

    // Create user
    const user = await prisma.user.create({
      data: {
        username: username.toLowerCase(),
        email: email ? email.toLowerCase() : null,
        passwordHash,
      }
    });

    // Generate JWT
    const token = jwt.sign(
      {
        type: 'user',
        userId: user.id,
        username: user.username,
      },
      JWT_SECRET,
      { expiresIn: '7d' }
    );

    return NextResponse.json({
      success: true,
      token,
      user: {
        id: user.id,
        username: user.username,
        email: user.email,
      }
    }, { status: 201 });

  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Signup error:", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Failed to create account" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}
```

### 4.2 POST /api/auth/login

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts`

```typescript
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
import bcrypt from "bcrypt";
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";

const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || "your-secret-key-change-in-production";

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  try {
    const { username, password } = await request.json();

    if (!username || !password) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Username and password are required" },
        { status: 400 }
      );
    }

    // Find user
    const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
      where: { username: username.toLowerCase() }
    });

    if (!user) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Invalid credentials" },
        { status: 401 }
      );
    }

    // Verify password
    const validPassword = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.passwordHash);

    if (!validPassword) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Invalid credentials" },
        { status: 401 }
      );
    }

    // Generate JWT
    const token = jwt.sign(
      {
        type: 'user',
        userId: user.id,
        username: user.username,
      },
      JWT_SECRET,
      { expiresIn: '7d' }
    );

    return NextResponse.json({
      success: true,
      token,
      user: {
        id: user.id,
        username: user.username,
        email: user.email,
      }
    });

  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Login error:", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Login failed" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}
```

### 4.3 GET /api/auth/me

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/api/auth/me/route.ts`

```typescript
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
import { verifyUserToken } from "@/lib/userAuth";

export async function GET(request: Request) {
  try {
    const authHeader = request.headers.get("authorization");
    
    if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "No token provided" },
        { status: 401 }
      );
    }

    const token = authHeader.replace("Bearer ", "");
    const payload = verifyUserToken(token);

    if (!payload) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Invalid or expired token" },
        { status: 401 }
      );
    }

    // Get fresh user data
    const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
      where: { id: payload.userId },
      select: {
        id: true,
        username: true,
        email: true,
        createdAt: true,
      }
    });

    if (!user) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "User not found" },
        { status: 404 }
      );
    }

    return NextResponse.json({ user });

  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Auth me error:", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Authentication check failed" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}
```

### 4.4 Keep Existing Admin Auth

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/api/auth/route.ts`

Keep this file unchanged. It handles admin authentication via env vars and returns base64 tokens.

---

## 5. Auth Utility Functions

### 5.1 Server-side Auth Utilities

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/lib/userAuth.ts`

```typescript
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || "your-secret-key-change-in-production";

export interface UserTokenPayload {
  type: 'user';
  userId: string;
  username: string;
  iat: number;
  exp: number;
}

export function verifyUserToken(token: string): UserTokenPayload | null {
  try {
    const payload = jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET) as UserTokenPayload;
    
    // Verify it's a user token, not an admin token
    if (payload.type !== 'user') {
      return null;
    }
    
    return payload;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

export function getUserFromRequest(request: Request): UserTokenPayload | null {
  const authHeader = request.headers.get("authorization");
  
  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
    return null;
  }

  const token = authHeader.replace("Bearer ", "");
  return verifyUserToken(token);
}

export function userUnauthorizedResponse() {
  return NextResponse.json(
    { error: "User authentication required" },
    { status: 401 }
  );
}

// Helper to check if request has valid user OR admin auth
export function checkUserOrAdmin(request: Request): { type: 'user' | 'admin', userId?: string } | null {
  // First check for user token
  const userPayload = getUserFromRequest(request);
  if (userPayload) {
    return { type: 'user', userId: userPayload.userId };
  }
  
  // Fall back to admin token check (existing logic)
  const token = request.headers.get("authorization");
  if (token) {
    try {
      const decoded = Buffer.from(token.replace("Bearer ", ""), "base64").toString();
      const [username, timestamp] = decoded.split(":");
      if (username && timestamp) {
        return { type: 'admin' };
      }
    } catch {
      // Not a valid admin token
    }
  }
  
  return null;
}
```

### 5.3 Client-side Auth Hook

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/hooks/useAuth.ts`

```typescript
'use client';

import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';

interface User {
  id: string;
  username: string;
  email: string | null;
}

interface AuthState {
  user: User | null;
  loading: boolean;
  error: string | null;
}

export function useAuth() {
  const [state, setState] = useState<AuthState>({
    user: null,
    loading: true,
    error: null,
  });

  const checkAuth = useCallback(async () => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem('user_token');
    
    if (!token) {
      setState({ user: null, loading: false, error: null });
      return;
    }

    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/auth/me', {
        headers: {
          'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`,
        },
      });

      if (response.ok) {
        const data = await response.json();
        setState({ user: data.user, loading: false, error: null });
      } else {
        // Token invalid, clear it
        localStorage.removeItem('user_token');
        setState({ user: null, loading: false, error: null });
      }
    } catch (error) {
      setState({ user: null, loading: false, error: 'Failed to check auth' });
    }
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    checkAuth();
  }, [checkAuth]);

  const login = async (username: string, password: string) => {
    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/auth/login', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ username, password }),
      });

      const data = await response.json();

      if (!response.ok) {
        throw new Error(data.error || 'Login failed');
      }

      localStorage.setItem('user_token', data.token);
      setState({ user: data.user, loading: false, error: null });
      return { success: true };
    } catch (error) {
      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Login failed';
      setState(prev => ({ ...prev, error: message }));
      return { success: false, error: message };
    }
  };

  const signup = async (username: string, password: string, email?: string) => {
    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/auth/signup', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ username, password, email }),
      });

      const data = await response.json();

      if (!response.ok) {
        throw new Error(data.error || 'Signup failed');
      }

      localStorage.setItem('user_token', data.token);
      setState({ user: data.user, loading: false, error: null });
      return { success: true };
    } catch (error) {
      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Signup failed';
      setState(prev => ({ ...prev, error: message }));
      return { success: false, error: message };
    }
  };

  const logout = () => {
    localStorage.removeItem('user_token');
    setState({ user: null, loading: false, error: null });
  };

  return {
    user: state.user,
    loading: state.loading,
    error: state.error,
    isAuthenticated: !!state.user,
    login,
    signup,
    logout,
    checkAuth,
  };
}
```

## 6. Frontend Pages

### 6.1 User Signup Page

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/signup/page.tsx`

```typescript
'use client';

import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';
import Link from 'next/link';
import { useAuth } from '@/hooks/useAuth';

export default function SignupPage() {
  const [username, setUsername] = useState('');
  const [password, setPassword] = useState('');
  const [confirmPassword, setConfirmPassword] = useState('');
  const [email, setEmail] = useState('');
  const [error, setError] = useState('');
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
  const router = useRouter();
  const { signup } = useAuth();

  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    setError('');

    if (password !== confirmPassword) {
      setError('Passwords do not match');
      return;
    }

    setIsLoading(true);

    const result = await signup(username, password, email || undefined);

    if (result.success) {
      router.push('/');
    } else {
      setError(result.error || 'Signup failed');
    }

    setIsLoading(false);
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-100 flex items-center justify-center">
      <div className="bg-white p-8 rounded-lg shadow-md w-96">
        <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6 text-center text-gray-900">Create Account</h1>
        
        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-4">
          <div>
            <label htmlFor="username" className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700">
              Username *
            </label>
            <input
              type="text"
              id="username"
              value={username}
              onChange={(e) => setUsername(e.target.value)}
              className="mt-1 block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-gray-900"
              required
              minLength={3}
              maxLength={30}
            />
          </div>

          <div>
            <label htmlFor="email" className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700">
              Email (optional - for password recovery)
            </label>
            <input
              type="email"
              id="email"
              value={email}
              onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
              className="mt-1 block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-gray-900"
            />
          </div>

          <div>
            <label htmlFor="password" className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700">
              Password *
            </label>
            <input
              type="password"
              id="password"
              value={password}
              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
              className="mt-1 block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-gray-900"
              required
              minLength={8}
            />
            <p className="text-xs text-gray-500 mt-1">At least 8 characters</p>
          </div>

          <div>
            <label htmlFor="confirmPassword" className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700">
              Confirm Password *
            </label>
            <input
              type="password"
              id="confirmPassword"
              value={confirmPassword}
              onChange={(e) => setConfirmPassword(e.target.value)}
              className="mt-1 block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-gray-900"
              required
            />
          </div>

          {error && (
            <div className="text-red-500 text-sm">{error}</div>
          )}

          <button
            type="submit"
            disabled={isLoading}
            className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white py-2 px-4 rounded-md hover:bg-blue-700 disabled:opacity-50"
          >
            {isLoading ? 'Creating Account...' : 'Sign Up'}
          </button>
        </form>

        <p className="mt-4 text-center text-sm text-gray-600">
          Already have an account?{' '}
          <Link href="/login" className="text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800">
            Log in
          </Link>
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

'use client';

import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';

import { useAuth } from '@/hooks/useAuth';

export default function LoginPage() {
  const [username, setUsername] = useState('');
  const [password, setPassword] = useState('');
  const [error, setError] = useState('');
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
  const router = useRouter();
  const { login } = useAuth();

  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    setError('');
    setIsLoading(true);

    const result = await login(username, password);

    if (result.success) {
      router.push('/');
    } else {
      setError(result.error || 'Login failed');
    }

    setIsLoading(false);
  };

  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-100 flex items-center justify-center">
      <div className="bg-white p-8 rounded-lg shadow-md w-96">
        <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6 text-center text-gray-900">Log In</h1>
        
        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-4">
          <div>
            <label htmlFor="username" className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700">
              Username
            </label>
            <input
              type="text"
              id="username"
              value={username}
              onChange={(e) => setUsername(e.target.value)}
              className="mt-1 block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-gray-900"
              required
            />
          </div>

          <div>
            <label htmlFor="password" className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700">
              Password
            </label>
            <input
              type="password"
              id="password"
              value={password}
              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
              className="mt-1 block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-gray-900"
              required
            />
          </div>

          {error && (
            <div className="text-red-500 text-sm">{error}</div>
          )}

          <button
            type="submit"
            disabled={isLoading}
            className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white py-2 px-4 rounded-md hover:bg-blue-700 disabled:opacity-50"
          >
            {isLoading ? 'Logging in...' : 'Log In'}
          </button>
        </form>

        <div className="mt-4 text-center space-y-2">
          <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">
            Don&apos;t have an account?{' '}
            <Link href="/signup" className="text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800">
              Sign up
            </Link>
          </p>
          <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">
            <Link href="/forgot-password" className="text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800">
              Forgot password?
            </Link>
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

### 6.3 Header User State Component

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/components/UserMenu.tsx`

```typescript
'use client';

import React, { useState } from 'react';
import Link from 'next/link';
import { useAuth } from '@/hooks/useAuth';
import { User, LogOut, ChevronDown } from 'lucide-react';

export default function UserMenu() {
  const { user, loading, isAuthenticated, logout } = useAuth();
  const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false);

  if (loading) {
    return (
      <div className="w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-gray-200 animate-pulse" />
    );
  }

  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return (
      <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
        <Link
          href="/login"
          className="text-white hover:text-blue-200 text-sm font-medium"
        >
          Log In
        </Link>
        <Link
          href="/signup"
          className="bg-white text-blue-600 px-3 py-1 rounded text-sm font-medium hover:bg-blue-50"
        >
          Sign Up
        </Link>
      </div>
    );
  }

  return (
    <div className="relative">
      <button
        onClick={() => setIsOpen(!isOpen)}
        className="flex items-center gap-2 text-white hover:text-blue-200"
      >
        <User className="w-5 h-5" />
        <span className="text-sm font-medium">{user?.username}</span>
        <ChevronDown className={`w-4 h-4 transition-transform ${isOpen ? 'rotate-180' : ''}`} />
      </button>

      {isOpen && (
        <div className="absolute right-0 mt-2 w-48 bg-white rounded-md shadow-lg py-1 z-50">
          <div className="px-4 py-2 border-b border-gray-100">
            <p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-900">{user?.username}</p>
            {user?.email && (
              <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">{user.email}</p>
            )}
          </div>
          <button
            onClick={() => {
              logout();
              setIsOpen(false);
            }}
            className="w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100 flex items-center gap-2"
          >
            <LogOut className="w-4 h-4" />
            Log Out
          </button>
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
```

## 10. Security Considerations

1. **Password hashing**: bcrypt with 12 salt rounds
2. **JWT secret**: Use a long, random string in production
3. **Token expiry**: 7 days for user tokens
4. **Rate limiting**: Consider adding for login/signup endpoints (future enhancement)
5. **HTTPS**: Ensure production uses HTTPS
6. **Input validation**: Username length, password strength
7. **Lowercase emails/usernames**: Prevent duplicate accounts

Design a user authentication system for a Next.js 15 app with Prisma/SQLite.

**Requirements:**
1. Signup/Login for public users (separate from existing admin auth)
2. Email is OPTIONAL - users can create accounts with just username/password
3. If email provided, it can be used for password recovery later
4. Keep existing admin auth system intact (env-based admin credentials)

**Current State:**
- Admin auth: POST /api/auth validates against ADMIN_USERNAME/ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars, returns base64 token stored in localStorage
- No User model in database (only Story, Category, Favorite models)

- Frontend uses Tailwind CSS, Lucide icons, localStorage for token storage

1. **Database Schema** - User model fields (id, username, email optional, passwordHash, createdAt)

2. **API Routes:**

   - POST /api/auth/login - authenticate user (keep admin auth at /api/auth)
   - GET /api/auth/me - validate token and return user info

3. **Password handling** - bcrypt for hashing

4. **Token strategy** - JWT or simpler approach? Consider:

5. **Frontend pages:**
   - /signup - registration form

6. **Auth utility functions** - for checking auth in API routes and frontend

     1→import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2→
     3→export async function POST(request: Request) {
     4→  try {
     5→    const { username, password } = await request.json();
     6→
     7→    const ADMIN_USERNAME = process.env.ADMIN_USERNAME;
     8→    const ADMIN_PASSWORD = process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD;
     9→
    10→    // If env vars not set, return error
    11→    if (!ADMIN_USERNAME || !ADMIN_PASSWORD) {
    12→      console.error(
    13→        "ADMIN_USERNAME or ADMIN_PASSWORD environment variables not set!",
    14→      );
    15→      return NextResponse.json(
    16→        { error: "Server configuration error" },
    17→        { status: 500 },
    18→      );
    19→    }
    20→
    21→    if (username === ADMIN_USERNAME && password === ADMIN_PASSWORD) {
    22→      // In production, generate a proper JWT token
    23→      const token = Buffer.from(`${username}:${Date.now()}`).toString("base64");
    24→
    25→      return NextResponse.json({
    26→        success: true,
    27→        token,
    28→        message: "Login successful",
    29→      });
    30→    } else {
    31→      return NextResponse.json(
    32→        { error: "Invalid credentials" },
    33→        { status: 401 },
    34→      );
    35→    }
    36→  } catch (error) {
    37→    console.error("Auth error:", error);
    38→    return NextResponse.json(
    39→      { error: "Authentication failed" },
    40→      { status: 500 },
    41→    );
    42→  }
    43→}
    44→

    23→      const response = await fetch('/api/auth', {
    24→        method: 'POST',
    25→        headers: {
    26→          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    27→        },
    28→        body: JSON.stringify({ username, password }),
    29→      });
    30→
    31→      const data = await response.json();
    32→
    33→      if (!response.ok) {
    34→        throw new Error(data.error || 'Login failed');
    35→      }
    36→
    37→      // Store auth token
    38→      localStorage.setItem('admin_token', data.token);
    39→      
    40→      // Redirect to admin dashboard on success
    41→      router.push(`/${secretPath}/dashboard`);
    42→    } catch (err) {
    43→      setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Login failed');
    44→    } finally {
    45→      setIsLoading(false);
    46→    }
    47→  };
    48→
    49→  return (
    50→    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gray-100 flex items-center justify-center">
    51→      <div className="bg-white p-8 rounded-lg shadow-md w-96">
    52→        <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6 text-center text-gray-900">Admin Login</h1>
    53→        
    54→        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-4">
    55→          <div>
    56→            <label htmlFor="username" className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700">
    57→              Username
    58→            </label>
    59→            <input
    60→              type="text"
    61→              id="username"
    62→              value={username}
    63→              onChange={(e) => setUsername(e.target.value)}
    64→              className="mt-1 block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-gray-900"
    65→              required
    66→            />
    67→          </div>
    68→
    69→          <div>
    70→            <label htmlFor="password" className="block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700">
    71→              Password
    72→            </label>
    73→            <input
    74→              type="password"
    75→              id="password"
    76→              value={password}
    77→              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
    78→              className="mt-1 block w-full rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-gray-900"
    79→              required
    80→            />
    81→          </div>
    82→
    83→          {error && (
    84→            <div className="text-red-500 text-sm">{error}</div>
    85→          )}
    86→
    87→          <button
    88→            type="submit"
    89→            disabled={isLoading}
    90→            className="w-full bg-blue-600 text-white py-2 px-4 rounded-md hover:bg-blue-700 disabled:opacity-50"
    91→          >
    92→            {isLoading ? 'Logging in...' : 'Login'}
    93→          </button>
    94→        </form>
    95→      </div>
    96→    </div>
    97→  );
    98→}
    99→
```

     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import React, {useState, useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';
     4→import { MoreHorizontal, ChevronDown } from 'lucide-react';
     5→
     6→interface ResponsiveNavProps {
     7→  categories?: string[];
     8→  activeTab?: string;
     9→  onTabChange: (category: string) => void;
    10→}
    11→
    12→const ResponsiveNav: React.FC<ResponsiveNavProps> = ({
    13→  categories = [],
    14→  activeTab = '',
    15→  onTabChange
    16→}) => {
    17→  const [overflowItems, setOverflowItems] = useState<string[]>([]);
    18→  const [visibleItems, setVisibleItems] = useState<string[]>(categories);
    19→  const [isOverflowOpen, setIsOverflowOpen] = useState(false);
    20→  const navRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
    21→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
    22→
    23→  const updateVisibleItems = useCallback(() => {
    24→    if (!navRef.current || !containerRef.current || !categories.length) return;
    25→
    26→    const OVERFLOW_BUTTON_WIDTH = 120;
    27→    const containerWidth = containerRef.current.offsetWidth - OVERFLOW_BUTTON_WIDTH;
    28→    const nav = navRef.current;
    29→    let totalWidth = 0;
    30→    const visible: string[] = [];
    31→    const overflow: string[] = [];
    32→
    33→    Array.from(nav.childNodes).forEach((child) => {
    34→      if (child instanceof HTMLElement) {
    35→        child.style.display = 'block';
    36→      }
    37→    });
    38→    
    39→    const BUTTON_PADDING = 8;
    40→    categories.forEach((category, index) => {
    41→      const button = nav.childNodes[index];
    42→      if (button instanceof HTMLElement) {
    43→        const buttonWidth = button.offsetWidth + BUTTON_PADDING;
    44→        if (totalWidth + buttonWidth < containerWidth) {
    45→          visible.push(category);
    46→          totalWidth += buttonWidth;
    47→        } else {
    48→          overflow.push(category);
    49→        }
    50→      }
    51→    });
    52→
    53→    setVisibleItems(visible);
    54→    setOverflowItems(overflow);
    55→  },[categories]);
    56→
    57→  useEffect(() => {
    58→    updateVisibleItems();
    59→  }, [updateVisibleItems]);
    60→
    61→  useEffect(() => {
    62→    const handleResize = () => {
    63→      updateVisibleItems();
    64→    };
    65→    window.addEventListener('resize', handleResize);
    66→    return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', handleResize);
    67→  }, [updateVisibleItems]);
    68→
    69→
    70→  if (!categories.length) {
    71→    return null;
    72→  }
    73→
    74→  return (
    75→    <div className="relative bg-white shadow sticky top-16 z-40" ref={containerRef}>
    76→      <div className="container mx-auto px-6">
    77→        <div className="flex items-center justify-between h-14">
    78→          <div className="flex-1 flex items-center gap-2 overflow-hidden" ref={navRef}>
    79→            {categories.map((category) => (
    80→              <button
    81→                key={category}
    82→                onClick={() => {
    83→                  onTabChange(category);
    84→                  setIsOverflowOpen(false);
    85→                }}
    86→                style={{ display: visibleItems.includes(category) ? 'block' : 'none' }}
    87→                className={`px-4 py-2 rounded-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-colors duration-200 ${
    88→                  activeTab === category
    89→                    ? 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-600'
    90→                    : 'text-gray-600 hover:bg-gray-100'
    91→                }`}
    92→              >
    93→                {category}
    94→              </button>
    95→            ))}
    96→          </div>
    97→
    98→          {overflowItems.length > 0 && (
    99→            <div className="relative ml-2">
   100→              <button
   101→                onClick={() => setIsOverflowOpen(!isOverflowOpen)}
   102→                className={`flex items-center px-3 py-2 rounded-sm font-medium transition-colors duration-200 
   103→                  ${isOverflowOpen ? 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-900' : 'text-gray-600 hover:bg-gray-100'}`}
   104→              >
   105→                <MoreHorizontal className="w-5 h-5" />
   106→                <ChevronDown className={`w-4 h-4 ml-1 transition-transform duration-200 
   107→                  ${isOverflowOpen ? 'rotate-180' : ''}`} />
   108→              </button>
   109→
   110→              {isOverflowOpen && (
   111→                <div className="absolute right-0 mt-2 py-2 w-48 bg-white rounded-md shadow-lg ring-1 ring-black ring-opacity-5 z-50">
   112→                  {overflowItems.map((category) => (
   113→                    <button
   114→                      key={category}
   115→                      onClick={() => {
   116→                        onTabChange(category);
   117→                        setIsOverflowOpen(false);
   118→                      }}
   119→                      className={`w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm transition-colors duration-200
   120→                        ${activeTab === category
   121→                          ? 'bg-blue-50 text-blue-600'
   122→                          : 'text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-50'
   123→                        }`}
   124→                    >
   125→                      {category}
   126→                    </button>
   127→                  ))}
   128→                </div>
   129→              )}
   130→            </div>
   131→          )}
   132→        </div>
   133→      </div>
   134→    </div>
   135→  );
   136→};
   137→
   138→export default ResponsiveNav;
   139→
   140→
```
</details></tool-use>

     4→
     5→export async function DELETE(
     6→  request: Request,
     7→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> },
     8→) {
     9→  // CHECK AUTHENTICATION FIRST!
    10→  if (!checkAuth(request)) {
    11→    return unauthorizedResponse();
    12→  }
    13→
    14→  try {
    15→    const { id } = await params;
    16→
    17→    await prisma.story.delete({
    18→      where: { id },
    19→    });
    20→
    21→    return NextResponse.json({
    22→      success: true,
    23→      message: "Story deleted successfully",
    24→    });
    25→  } catch (error) {

    27→    return NextResponse.json(
    28→      { error: "Failed to delete story" },
    29→      { status: 500 },
    30→    );
    31→  }
    32→}
    33→
    34→export async function PATCH(
    35→  request: Request,
    36→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> },
    37→) {
    38→  // CHECK AUTHENTICATION FIRST!
    39→  if (!checkAuth(request)) {
    40→    return unauthorizedResponse();
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  try {
    44→    const { id } = await params;
    45→    const body = await request.json();
    46→
    47→    // only include valid story fields
    48→    const updateData: {
    49→      title?: string;
    50→      url?: string;
    51→      category?: string;
    52→      description?: string | null;
    53→      author?: string | null;
    54→      publicationMonth?: number | null;
    55→      publicationYear?: number | null;
    56→    } = {};
    57→
    58→    if (body.title !== undefined) updateData.title = body.title;
    59→    if (body.url !== undefined) updateData.url = body.url;
    60→    if (body.category !== undefined) updateData.category = body.category;
    61→    if (body.description !== undefined)
    62→      updateData.description = body.description;
    63→    if (body.author !== undefined) updateData.author = body.author;
    64→    if (body.publicationMonth !== undefined)
    65→      updateData.publicationMonth = body.publicationMonth;
    66→    if (body.publicationYear !== undefined)
    67→      updateData.publicationYear = body.publicationYear;
    68→
    69→    const updatedStory = await prisma.story.update({
    70→      where: { id },
    71→      data: updateData,
    72→    });
    73→
    74→    return NextResponse.json(updatedStory);
    75→  } catch (error) {
    76→    console.error("Error updating story:", error);
    77→    return NextResponse.json(
    78→      { error: "Failed to update story" },
    79→      { status: 500 },
    80→    );
    81→  }
    82→}

     1→import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2→import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
     3→import { headers } from "next/headers";
     4→
     5→// Get user identifier (IP address for now)
     6→async function getUserId() {
     7→  const headersList = await headers();
     8→  const forwarded = headersList.get("x-forwarded-for");
     9→  const ip = forwarded
    10→    ? forwarded.split(",")[0]
    11→    : headersList.get("x-real-ip") || "unknown";
    12→  return `user_${ip}`;
    13→}
    14→
    15→// GET - Get all favorites for current user
    16→export async function GET() {
    17→  try {
    18→    const userId = await getUserId();
    19→
    20→    const favorites = await prisma.favorite.findMany({
    21→      where: {
    22→        userId,
    23→      },
    24→      select: {
    25→        storyId: true,
    26→      },
    27→    });
    28→
    29→    const favoriteIds = favorites.map((f: { storyId: string }) => f.storyId);
    30→
    31→    return NextResponse.json({ favoriteIds });
    32→  } catch (error) {
    33→    console.error("Error fetching favorites:", error);
    34→    return NextResponse.json(
    35→      { error: "Failed to fetch favorites" },
    36→      { status: 500 },
    37→    );
    38→  }
    39→}
    40→
    41→// POST - Add a favorite (NO AUTH REQUIRED - public feature)
    42→export async function POST(request: Request) {
    43→  try {
    44→    const userId = await getUserId();
    45→    const body = await request.json();
    46→    const { storyId } = body;
    47→
    48→    if (!storyId) {
    49→      return NextResponse.json(
    50→        { error: "Story ID is required" },
    51→        { status: 400 },
    52→      );
    53→    }
    54→
    55→    // Check if already favorited
    56→    const existing = await prisma.favorite.findUnique({
    57→      where: {
    58→        storyId_userId: {
    59→          storyId,
    60→          userId,
    61→        },
    62→      },
    63→    });
    64→
    65→    if (existing) {
    66→      return NextResponse.json({
    67→        message: "Already favorited",
    68→        favorited: true,
    69→      });
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // Create favorite
    73→    await prisma.favorite.create({
    74→      data: {
    75→        storyId,
    76→        userId,
    77→      },
    78→    });
    79→
    80→    return NextResponse.json({
    81→      message: "Favorite added",
    82→      favorited: true,
    83→    });
    84→  } catch (error) {
    85→    console.error("Error adding favorite:", error);
    86→    return NextResponse.json(
    87→      { error: "Failed to add favorite" },
    88→      { status: 500 },
    89→    );
    90→  }
    91→}
    92→
    93→// DELETE - Remove a favorite (NO AUTH REQUIRED - public feature)
    94→export async function DELETE(request: Request) {
    95→  try {
    96→    const userId = await getUserId();
    97→    const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url);
    98→    const storyId = searchParams.get("storyId");
    99→
   100→    if (!storyId) {
   101→      return NextResponse.json(
   102→        { error: "Story ID is required" },
   103→        { status: 400 },
   104→      );
   105→    }
   106→
   107→    await prisma.favorite.deleteMany({
   108→      where: {
   109→        storyId,
   110→        userId,
   111→      },
   112→    });
   113→
   114→    return NextResponse.json({
   115→      message: "Favorite removed",
   116→      favorited: false,
   117→    });
   118→  } catch (error) {
   119→    console.error("Error removing favorite:", error);
   120→    return NextResponse.json(
   121→      { error: "Failed to remove favorite" },
   122→      { status: 500 },
   123→    );
   124→  }
   125→}
   126→

     1→// src/lib/auth.ts
     2→import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     3→
     4→export function checkAuth(request: Request): boolean {
     5→  const token = request.headers.get("authorization");
     6→
     7→  if (!token) {
     8→    return false;
     9→  }
    10→
    11→  // Basic token validation
    12→  // In production, verify JWT or session token properly
    13→  try {
    14→    const decoded = Buffer.from(
    15→      token.replace("Bearer ", ""),
    16→      "base64",
    17→    ).toString();
    18→    // Token format: "username:timestamp"
    19→    const [username, timestamp] = decoded.split(":");
    20→
    21→    // Check if token is from valid admin session
    22→    // For now, just check if it's properly formatted
    23→    return !!username && !!timestamp;
    24→  } catch {
    25→    return false;
    26→  }
    27→}
    28→
    29→export function unauthorizedResponse() {
    30→  return NextResponse.json(
    31→    { error: "Unauthorized - Admin access required" },
    32→    { status: 401 },
    33→  );
    34→}
    35→

     7→interface Story {
     8→  id: string;
     9→  title: string;
    10→  url: string;

    18→
    19→export default function FutureNews() {

    22→    Story[]
    23→  >([]);
    24→  const [filteredStories, setFilteredStories] = useState<Story[]>([]);
    25→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    26→  const [favorites, setFavorites] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set());
    27→
    28→  // Category/Tab states
    29→  const [categories, setCategories] = useState<string[]>([]);
    30→  const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<string>("All");
    31→
    32→  // Search states
    33→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState("");
    34→  const [searchInTitle, setSearchInTitle] = useState(true);
    35→  const [searchInDescription, setSearchInDescription] = useState(true);
    36→  const [searchInCategory, setSearchInCategory] = useState(false);
    37→  const [searchInAuthor, setSearchInAuthor] = useState(false);
    38→  const [caseSensitive, setCaseSensitive] = useState(false);
    39→  const [wholeWords, setWholeWords] = useState(false);
    40→
    41→  // Favorites filter
    42→  const [showOnlyFavorites, setShowOnlyFavorites] = useState(false);
    43→
    44→  // Sort states
    45→  const [sortField, setSortField] = useState<"date" | "title">("date");
    46→  const [sortDirection, setSortDirection] = useState<"asc" | "desc">("desc");
    47→
    48→  useEffect(() => {
    49→    fetchStories();
    50→    fetchFavorites();
    51→  }, []);
    52→
    53→  const fetchStories = async () => {
    54→    try {
    55→      setLoading(true);
    56→      const response = await fetch("/api/stories");
    57→      if (response.ok) {
    58→        const data = await response.json();
    59→        setAllStories(data);
    60→
    61→        // Extract unique categories and add "All" at the beginning
    62→        const uniqueCategories = Array.from(
    63→          new Set(data.map((s: Story) => s.category)),
    64→        ) as string[];
    65→        setCategories(["All", ...uniqueCategories.sort()]);
    66→
    67→        // Initially show all stories
    68→        setCategoryFilteredStories(data);
    69→        setFilteredStories(data);
    70→      }
    71→    } catch (error) {
    72→      console.error("Failed to fetch stories:", error);
    73→    } finally {
    74→      setLoading(false);
    75→    }
    76→  };
    77→
    78→  const fetchFavorites = async () => {
    79→    try {
    80→      const response = await fetch("/api/favorites");
    81→      if (response.ok) {
    82→        const data = await response.json();
    83→        setFavorites(new Set(data.favoriteIds));
    84→      }
    85→    } catch (error) {
    86→      console.error("Failed to fetch favorites:", error);
    87→    }
    88→  };
    89→
    90→  const toggleFavorite = async (storyId: string) => {
    91→    const isFavorited = favorites.has(storyId);
    92→
    93→    // Optimistic update
    94→    const newFavorites = new Set(favorites);
    95→    if (isFavorited) {
    96→      newFavorites.delete(storyId);
    97→    } else {
    98→      newFavorites.add(storyId);
    99→    }
   100→    setFavorites(newFavorites);
   101→
   102→    try {
   103→      if (isFavorited) {
   104→        await fetch(`/api/favorites?storyId=${storyId}`, {
   105→          method: "DELETE",
   106→        });
   107→      } else {
   108→        await fetch("/api/favorites", {
   109→          method: "POST",
   110→          headers: {

   118→      setFavorites(favorites);
   119→    }
   120→  };
   121→
   122→  // Filter by category when active tab changes
   123→  useEffect(() => {
   124→    let filtered: Story[];
   125→
   126→    if (activeTab === "All") {
   127→      filtered = allStories;
   128→    } else {
   129→      filtered = allStories.filter((story) => story.category === activeTab);
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    setCategoryFilteredStories(filtered);
   133→  }, [activeTab, allStories]);
   134→
   135→  // Apply search filter on category-filtered stories
   136→  useEffect(() => {
   137→    let result = categoryFilteredStories;
   138→
   139→    // Apply search filter
   140→    if (searchQuery.trim()) {
   141→      result = result.filter((story) => {
   142→        const fieldsToSearch: string[] = [];
   143→
   144→        if (searchInTitle && story.title) {
   145→          fieldsToSearch.push(story.title);
   146→        }
   147→
   148→        if (searchInDescription && story.description) {
   149→          fieldsToSearch.push(story.description);
   150→        }
   151→
   152→        if (searchInCategory && story.category) {
   153→          fieldsToSearch.push(story.category);
   154→        }
   155→
   156→        if (searchInAuthor && story.author) {
   157→          fieldsToSearch.push(story.author);
   158→        }
   159→
   160→        if (fieldsToSearch.length === 0) {
   161→          return false;
   162→        }
   163→
   164→        let pattern: RegExp;
   165→
   166→        if (wholeWords) {
   167→          const escapedQuery = searchQuery.replace(

   169→            "\\$&",
   170→          );
   171→          const regexFlags = caseSensitive ? "g" : "gi";
   172→          pattern = new RegExp(`\\b${escapedQuery}\\b`, regexFlags);
   173→        } else {
   174→          const regexFlags = caseSensitive ? "g" : "gi";
   175→          const escapedQuery = searchQuery.replace(
   176→            /[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g,
   177→            "\\$&",
   178→          );
   179→          pattern = new RegExp(escapedQuery, regexFlags);
   180→        }
   181→
   182→        return fieldsToSearch.some((field) => {
   183→          if (wholeWords || !caseSensitive) {
   184→            return pattern.test(field);
   185→          } else {
   186→            return field.includes(searchQuery);
   187→          }
   188→        });
   189→      });

   192→    // Apply favorites filter

   209→        } else {
   210→          return titleB.localeCompare(titleA);
   211→        }
   212→      }
   213→    });

## User Authentication System Design Plan

Based on my analysis of the codebase, here is a comprehensive implementation plan for adding user authentication to your Next.js 15 app.

---

## 1. Database Schema

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/prisma/schema.prisma`

Add the following User model and PasswordResetToken model:

```prisma
model User {
  id            String    @id @default(cuid())
  username      String    @unique
  email         String?   @unique  // Optional, for password recovery
  passwordHash  String
  createdAt     DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime  @updatedAt
  
  // Relation to favorites (replacing IP-based identification)
  favorites     UserFavorite[]
  
  @@index([email])
}

model PasswordResetToken {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  token     String   @unique
  userId    String
  expiresAt DateTime
  used      Boolean  @default(false)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@index([token])
  @@index([userId])
}

// Optional: Convert Favorite to use User relation
model UserFavorite {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  storyId   String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  story     Story    @relation(fields: [storyId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@unique([userId, storyId])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([storyId])
}
```

**Note:** The existing `Favorite` model can remain for anonymous users (IP-based), or you can migrate to `UserFavorite` for logged-in users.

---

## 2. Dependencies

Add bcrypt for password hashing and jsonwebtoken for JWT:

```bash
npm install bcrypt jsonwebtoken
npm install -D @types/bcrypt @types/jsonwebtoken
```

---

## 3. Token Strategy

### Recommended: JWT-based Authentication

**Why JWT over simple base64:**
- Cryptographically signed (tamper-proof)
- Built-in expiration
- Can contain user info (userId, username)
- Easy to differentiate from admin tokens

**Token Structure:**

```typescript
// User token payload
interface UserTokenPayload {
  type: 'user';           // Differentiates from admin
  userId: string;
  username: string;
  iat: number;            // Issued at
  exp: number;            // Expiration (7 days recommended)
}

// Admin token remains base64 for backward compatibility
// Admin token format: "admin:username:timestamp" base64 encoded
```

**Storage:**
- User tokens stored in `localStorage` as `user_token` (separate from `admin_token`)
- Or use a single `auth_token` key with type checking on the server

---

## 4. API Routes

### 4.1 POST /api/auth/signup

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/api/auth/signup/route.ts`

```typescript
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
import bcrypt from "bcrypt";
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";

const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || "your-secret-key-change-in-production";
const SALT_ROUNDS = 12;

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  try {
    const { username, password, email } = await request.json();

    // Validation
    if (!username || !password) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Username and password are required" },
        { status: 400 }
      );
    }

    if (username.length < 3 || username.length > 30) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Username must be 3-30 characters" },
        { status: 400 }
      );
    }

    if (password.length < 8) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Password must be at least 8 characters" },
        { status: 400 }
      );
    }

    // Check if username exists
    const existingUser = await prisma.user.findUnique({
      where: { username: username.toLowerCase() }
    });

    if (existingUser) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Username already taken" },
        { status: 409 }
      );
    }

    // Check if email exists (if provided)
    if (email) {
      const existingEmail = await prisma.user.findUnique({
        where: { email: email.toLowerCase() }
      });
      if (existingEmail) {
        return NextResponse.json(
          { error: "Email already registered" },
          { status: 409 }
        );
      }
    }

    // Hash password
    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, SALT_ROUNDS);

    // Create user
    const user = await prisma.user.create({
      data: {
        username: username.toLowerCase(),
        email: email ? email.toLowerCase() : null,
        passwordHash,
      }
    });

    // Generate JWT
    const token = jwt.sign(
      {
        type: 'user',
        userId: user.id,
        username: user.username,
      },
      JWT_SECRET,
      { expiresIn: '7d' }
    );

    return NextResponse.json({
      success: true,
      token,
      user: {
        id: user.id,
        username: user.username,
        email: user.email,
      }
    }, { status: 201 });

  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Signup error:", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Failed to create account" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}
```

### 4.2 POST /api/auth/login

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts`

```typescript
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
import bcrypt from "bcrypt";
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";

const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || "your-secret-key-change-in-production";

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  try {
    const { username, password } = await request.json();

    if (!username || !password) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Username and password are required" },
        { status: 400 }
      );
    }

    // Find user
    const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
      where: { username: username.toLowerCase() }
    });

    if (!user) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Invalid credentials" },
        { status: 401 }
      );
    }

    // Verify password
    const validPassword = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.passwordHash);

    if (!validPassword) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Invalid credentials" },
        { status: 401 }
      );
    }

    // Generate JWT
    const token = jwt.sign(
      {
        type: 'user',
        userId: user.id,
        username: user.username,
      },
      JWT_SECRET,
      { expiresIn: '7d' }
    );

    return NextResponse.json({
      success: true,
      token,
      user: {
        id: user.id,
        username: user.username,
        email: user.email,
      }
    });

  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Login error:", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Login failed" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}
```

### 4.3 GET /api/auth/me

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/app/api/auth/me/route.ts`

```typescript
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
import { verifyUserToken } from "@/lib/userAuth";

export async function GET(request: Request) {
  try {
    const authHeader = request.headers.get("authorization");
    
    if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "No token provided" },
        { status: 401 }
      );
    }

    const token = authHeader.replace("Bearer ", "");
    const payload = verifyUserToken(token);

    if (!payload) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "Invalid or expired token" },
        { status: 401 }
      );
    }

    // Get fresh user data
    const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
      where: { id: payload.userId },
      select: {
        id: true,
        username: true,
        email: true,
        createdAt: true,
      }
    });

    if (!user) {
      return NextResponse.json(
        { error: "User not found" },
        { status: 404 }
      );
    }

    return NextResponse.json({ user });

  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Auth me error:", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Authentication check failed" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}
```

## 5. Auth Utility Functions

### 5.1 Server-side Auth Utilities

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/lib/userAuth.ts`

```typescript
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || "your-secret-key-change-in-production";

export interface UserTokenPayload {
  type: 'user';
  userId: string;
  username: string;
  iat: number;
  exp: number;
}

export function verifyUserToken(token: string): UserTokenPayload | null {
  try {
    const payload = jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET) as UserTokenPayload;
    
    // Verify it's a user token, not an admin token
    if (payload.type !== 'user') {
      return null;
    }
    
    return payload;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

export function getUserFromRequest(request: Request): UserTokenPayload | null {
  const authHeader = request.headers.get("authorization");
  
  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
    return null;
  }

  const token = authHeader.replace("Bearer ", "");
  return verifyUserToken(token);
}

export function userUnauthorizedResponse() {
  return NextResponse.json(
    { error: "User authentication required" },
    { status: 401 }
  );
}

// Helper to check if request has valid user OR admin auth
export function checkUserOrAdmin(request: Request): { type: 'user' | 'admin', userId?: string } | null {
  // First check for user token
  const userPayload = getUserFromRequest(request);
  if (userPayload) {
    return { type: 'user', userId: userPayload.userId };
  }
  
  // Fall back to admin token check (existing logic)
  const token = request.headers.get("authorization");
  if (token) {
    try {
      const decoded = Buffer.from(token.replace("Bearer ", ""), "base64").toString();
      const [username, timestamp] = decoded.split(":");
      if (username && timestamp) {
        return { type: 'admin' };
      }
    } catch {
      // Not a valid admin token
    }
  }
  
  return null;
}
```

### 5.3 Client-side Auth Hook

**File:** `/home/rick/Documents/D/PRJ/LRG/futureisnear/src/hooks/useAuth.ts`

```typescript
'use client';

import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';

interface User {
  id: string;
  username: string;
  email: string | null;
}

interface AuthState {
  user: User | null;
  loading: boolean;
  error: string | null;
}

export function useAuth() {
  const [state, setState] = useState<AuthState>({
    user: null,
    loading: true,
    error: null,
  });

  const checkAuth = useCallback(async () => {
    const token = localStorage.getItem('user_token');
    
    if (!token) {
      setState({ user: null, loading: false, error: null });
      return;
    }

    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/auth/me', {
        headers: {
          'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`,
        },
      });

      if (response.ok) {
        const data = await response.json();
        setState({ user: data.user, loading: false, error: null });
      } else {
        // Token invalid, clear it
        localStorage.removeItem('user_token');
        setState({ user: null, loading: false, error: null });
      }
    } catch (error) {
      setState({ user: null, loading: false, error: 'Failed to check auth' });
    }
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    checkAuth();
  }, [checkAuth]);

  const login = async (username: string, password: string) => {
    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/auth/login', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ username, password }),
      });

      const data = await response.json();

      if (!response.ok) {
        throw new Error(data.error || 'Login failed');
      }

      localStorage.setItem('user_token', data.token);
      setState({ user: data.user, loading: false, error: null });
      return { success: true };
    } catch (error) {
      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Login failed';
      setState(prev => ({ ...prev, error: message }));
      return { success: false, error: message };
    }
  };

  const signup = async (username: string, password: string, email?: string) => {
    try {
      const response = await fetch('/api/auth/signup', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ username, password, email }),
      });

      const data = await response.json();

      if (!response.ok) {
        throw new Error(data.error || 'Signup failed');
      }

      localStorage.setItem('user_token', data.token);
      setState({ user: data.user, loading: false, error: null });
      return { success: true };
    } catch (error) {
      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Signup failed';
      setState(prev => ({ ...prev, error: message }));
      return { success: false, error: message };
    }
  };

  const logout = () => {
    localStorage.removeItem('user_token');
    setState({ user: null, loading: false, error: null });
  };

  return {
    user: state.user,
    loading: state.loading,
    error: state.error,
    isAuthenticated: !!state.user,
    login,
    signup,
    logout,
    checkAuth,
  };
}
```
